title: Executive Order 7970—Regulations Governing the Grades and Ratings of Enlisted Men of the Regular Army for the Fiscal Year 1939
text:
By virtue of and pursuant to the authority vested in me by the act of June 20, 1936, 49 Stat. 1554, it is ordered that during the fiscal year 1939 the grades and ratings of the enlisted men of the Regular Army shall be as set forth herein, and that the number of enlisted men in the several grades and ratings shall not exceed the number specified herein.
1. The several grades and the maximum number of enlisted men therein shall be as follows:
Number
1st Grade—Master Sergeants--------------------------
1,170
2d Grade—First Sergeants and Technical Sergeants----
3,133
3d Grade—Staff Sergeants-----------------------------
5,055
4th Grade—Sergeants----------------------------------
14,836
5th Grade—Corporals----------------------------------
14,285
6th Grade—Privates, 1st Class-------------------------
44,488
7th Grade—Privates, the number of whom will be such that when added to the number of enlisted men above Grade Seven and to the authorized number of flying cadets the total will not exceed the enlisted pay strength of the Army appropriated for by the "Military Appropriation Act, 1939", approved June 11, 1938.
2. Specialists ratings and the maximum number of enlisted men therein shall be as follows:
Number
1st Class---------------------------------------------
909
2nd Class--------------------------------------------
1,549
3rd Class--------------------------------------------
2,437
4th Class--------------------------------------------
6,797
5th Class--------------------------------------------
7,120
6th Class--------------------------------------------
19,472
FRANKLIN D ROOSEVELT
The White House,
September 12, 1938.
